name: Piedrahíta — circular by the Palacio Duques de Alba
name_original: Piedrahíta — circular por el Palacio Duques de Alba
region_intro: Jogging routes around Barco Avila-Piedrahita traverse a diverse landscape, from the high peaks of the Sierra de Gredos to the gentle river valleys. The region is characterized by rugged mountains, deep gorges, and the Tormes River, which offers flatter, more accessible paths. Historical towns like El Barco de Ávila provide urban running opportunities alongside natural trails. This varied terrain caters to a wide range of running preferences and fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Barco Avila-Piedrahita The most popular jogging route is Castillo de El Barco de Ávila — circular por alrededores del Río Tromes, an easy 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes about 34 minutes to complete. This route follows the Tormes River, offering a refreshing environment with minimal elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Circos de Nava y de la Laguna del Barco — circular por la Cuerda de Riscos Altos - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos, a difficult 14.4 miles (23.2 km) path. This challenging trail explores the high-altitude landscapes of the Sierra de Gredos, featuring significant ascents and panoramic views. Local runners also love the Piedrahíta — circular by the Palacio Duques de Alba, a 1.9 miles (3.1 km) trail leading through the historic town of Piedrahíta, often completed in about 21 minutes. Jogging around Barco Avila-Piedrahita is defined by mountain trails in the Sierra de Gredos, riverside paths along the Tormes, and routes through historic towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle runs to challenging trail running. The routes in Barco Avila-Piedrahita are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Barco Avila-Piedrahita's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running trails are available in the Barco Avila-Piedrahita region?
A: The Barco Avila-Piedrahita region offers a wide selection of running routes. You can find over 25 different trails, catering to various preferences and fitness levels, from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes around Barco Avila-Piedrahita?
A: The region boasts diverse terrain. You'll find routes traversing the rugged mountains of the Sierra de Gredos, gentle riverside paths along the Tormes and Adaja rivers, and trails through historic towns like El Barco de Ávila and Piedrahíta. Expect a mix of paved surfaces, natural trails, and some rocky sections, especially in higher elevations.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in this area?
A: Yes, there are several easy running routes perfect for beginners. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1165074004/castillo-de-el-barco-de-avila-circular-por-alrededores-del-rio-tromes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de El Barco de Ávila — circular por alrededores del Río Tromes&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.1-mile (5.0 km) trail along the Tormes River with minimal elevation gain. Another accessible choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1165810673/piedrahita-circular-by-the-palacio-duques-de-alba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrahíta — circular by the Palacio Duques de Alba&lt;/a&gt;, a 1.9-mile (3.1 km) route through the historic town of Piedrahíta.

Q: What are some challenging trail running options in Barco Avila-Piedrahita?
A: For experienced trail runners seeking a challenge, the region offers demanding routes, particularly in the Sierra de Gredos. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1158226849/circos-de-nava-y-de-la-laguna-del-barco-circular-por-la-cuerda-de-riscos-altos-parque-regional-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circos de Nava y de la Laguna del Barco — circular por la Cuerda de Riscos Altos - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.4-mile (23.2 km) path with significant ascents and panoramic views. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159404372/source-of-the-adaja-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Adaja River Trail&lt;/a&gt;, covering 8.8 miles (14.2 km) with over 500 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in the Barco Avila-Piedrahita region are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1165074004/castillo-de-el-barco-de-avila-circular-por-alrededores-del-rio-tromes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de El Barco de Ávila — circular por alrededores del Río Tromes&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1158226849/circos-de-nava-y-de-la-laguna-del-barco-circular-por-la-cuerda-de-riscos-altos-parque-regional-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circos de Nava y de la Laguna del Barco — circular por la Cuerda de Riscos Altos - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Barco Avila-Piedrahita?
A: The running trails in Barco Avila-Piedrahita are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the stunning natural beauty, the variety of terrain, and the well-maintained paths that offer both physical challenge and aesthetic pleasure.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by historical landmarks or interesting sights?
A: Absolutely! The region is rich in history. You can run through the medieval town of El Barco de Ávila, passing its 12th-century castle and Romanesque bridge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31430069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Barco de Ávila – Valdecorneja Castle loop from El Barco de Ávila&lt;/a&gt; is a great option. In Piedrahíta, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1165810673/piedrahita-circular-by-the-palacio-duques-de-alba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrahíta — circular by the Palacio Duques de Alba&lt;/a&gt; take you past the historic Palacio Duques de Alba.

Q: Are there any jogging paths that feature waterfalls or natural gorges?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Gredos offers impressive natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2654537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de la Nava&lt;/a&gt; is a notable gorge with rock formations and waterfalls, featuring a stone walkway that provides a scenic and engaging route. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2653775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Nava&lt;/a&gt; in this area, accessible via routes through the gorge.

Q: Where can I find routes along the Tormes River?
A: The Tormes River offers flatter, more accessible paths ideal for leisurely jogs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1165074004/castillo-de-el-barco-de-avila-circular-por-alrededores-del-rio-tromes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de El Barco de Ávila — circular por alrededores del Río Tromes&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent easy running trail that follows the river, providing a refreshing environment with minimal elevation gain. Many other sections of the Tormes River also feature routes with low elevation and often paved surfaces.

Q: Are there family-friendly running routes in Barco Avila-Piedrahita?
A: For families, the easier, flatter routes along the Tormes River are ideal. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1165074004/castillo-de-el-barco-de-avila-circular-por-alrededores-del-rio-tromes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de El Barco de Ávila — circular por alrededores del Río Tromes&lt;/a&gt; is a great choice, offering a pleasant and manageable distance for all ages. These routes often feature paved surfaces and beautiful scenery without significant climbs.

Q: Are the running trails in Barco Avila-Piedrahita dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in the Barco Avila-Piedrahita region are suitable for running with dogs, especially the natural paths and riverside routes. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in protected areas like the Sierra de Gredos, and to be mindful of local regulations and other trail users. Always carry water for yourself and your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Barco Avila-Piedrahita?
A: The best time for jogging in Barco Avila-Piedrahita is generally during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially in the valleys, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable. Winter offers crisp air, but higher mountain trails may be inaccessible due to snow.
tips: This is one of the most important buildings in the town of Pedrahíta along with the Palace of the Dukes of Alba and the Convento de Carmelitas which is located in the same town.
Huge park that is located in front of the palace of the Dukes of Alba. A large palace surrounded by walls and which is difficult not to see as it is one of the important points of the town.
